g.text: Character extraction retrieves individual characters or character sequences from strings using
methods like charAt(), toCharArray(), getChars(), and substring().
data-narration="Character extraction is the process of pulling out individual characters or groups of characters from a String for analysis and manipulation."
data-narration="Character extraction powers data parsing, validation, and text analysis. Java provides multiple methods for different extraction needs."
data-narration="charAt(index) returns the character at the specified index. Java uses zero-based indexing, so the first character is at index 0."
data-narration="Use charAt() in a loop to iterate through each character. Compare each character to a target and count matches."
char ch = str.charAt(5); // Error if len=5
data-narration="Always validate indices before calling charAt(). The exception occurs if index is negative or greater than or equal to length()."
toCharArray() - Convert String to Character Array
data-narration="toCharArray() converts the entire string into a new character array. Modifying the array doesn't affect the original string."
data-narration="getChars() efficiently copies a range of characters into a destination array. More efficient than multiple charAt() calls."
data-narration="getBytes() returns the byte representation of the string using UTF-8 encoding. Essential for I/O operations and network transmission."
data-narration="substring(start, end) extracts characters from start index up to but not including end index. Returns a new String object."
data-narration="String is immutable - extraction methods return new objects. StringBuffer is mutable and allows in-place modifications."
data-narration="charAt() is O(1) for single access. For ranges, getChars() is more efficient than multiple charAt() calls in a loop."
data-narration="Master these four methods for character extraction. Remember: zero-based indexing, handle exceptions, and choose the right method for your use case."
